description: Colombian criminal organization active from 1970 to 2010, based in Bogotá. It supplied narcotics to the Medellín Cartel and later became one of the world's largest after the decline of the Medellín and Cali cartels. Led by Justo Pastor Perafán and later Luis Caicedo, it engaged in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, human trafficking, and arms trafficking. The organization was dismantled after Caicedo's arrest in 2010.

country_links.evidence_quote: Hernán Uribe: Miembro. Manejaba las rutas desde Venezuela y Ecuador hacía México y España.
country_links.context: The Cartel del Dorado managed routes from Ecuador.

country_links.evidence_quote: Hernán Uribe: Miembro. Manejaba las rutas desde Venezuela y Ecuador hacía México y España.
country_links.context: The Cartel del Dorado managed routes to Mexico.

country_links.evidence_quote: Alfonso León: delegado del cartel de Bogotá en Europa, basado en España.
country_links.context: The Cartel del Dorado had delegates based in Spain.

country_links.evidence_quote: Estuvo preso 70 meses en una cárcel de Estados Unidos por obstrucción a la justicia al darle aviso con antelación a un narco de que iba a ser extraditado a Estados Unidos.
country_links.context: A member of the Cartel del Dorado was imprisoned in the United States for obstruction of justice related to warning a drug trafficker about extradition.
